fault_hypothesis: The expiration check on line 67 uses a strict greater-than comparison (`>`), but it should be greater-than-or-equal-to (`>=`). This causes a token to be considered valid when the current time is exactly equal to its effective TTL, when it should be considered expired.

constraints.intent: A token should only be considered valid if the current time is strictly less than its effective TTL.
The effective TTL, including clock skew, should not be earlier than the base TTL.
A valid cache item must be a tuple of two elements (payload, ttl) before it is unpacked.
